Idaho Power reports over 150 customers without power from the west side of Chubbuck west to American Falls Reservoir. Power interruptions observed at NWS Pocatello office with severe winds over 60 MPH.
Idaho Power reports over 400 customers without power from Gooding into far southwest Lincoln County.

ITD Gem Valley.
Idaho Power reports over 80 customers without power between Aberdeen and American Falls Reservoir.
Visibility 2 miles or less from 1040 AM through 1125 AM at KIDA Idaho Falls Regional Airport with blowing dust observed on webcams. Winds gusting 45 to 55 MPH during this period.
Power County Sheriffs Office reports high winds and limited visibility along ID-39 in the area of Lamb Weston Rd and Vollmer Loop, and requests caution for anyone traveling through the area.
Significant blowing dust across I-15 near Shelley.
Significant blowing dust reported across I-15 in the area of the Blackfoot Rest Area. Reduced visibility confirmed by ITD RWIS webcams with winds gusting 45-50 MPH.
Date corrected...Significant blowing dust across US-26 near Ririe with reduced visibility. Photo via social media.
Significant blowing dust across I-15 just north of Fort Hall.
Photo from the public of blowing dust over American Falls Reservoir, taken near the dam. Visibility estimated at 1/2 mile or less.

Blowing dust across the lower Snake Plain and I-15 corridor near the north end of Arbon Valley per NWS employee reports and ITD RWIS webcam imagery throughout the late morning and early afternoon hours. Worst area visibility so far occurred around 135 PM with winds gusting to 45 MPH.
Photo from the public of blowing dust across I-15 near Exit 80 Fort Hall with visibility estimated at 1/2 mile.
Significant blowing dust across the area seen from I-15 just south of Exit 108.
Video of significant blowing dust continuing across ID-39 from American Falls Reservoir dam north to S Pleasant Valley Rd. Visibility near zero in spots.
Significant blowing dust across US-26 near Ririe with reduced visibility. Photo via social media.
Tom Cat Summit ITD site.

Jefferson County 911 Dispatch reports widespread wind damage across the eastern half of Jefferson County, including multiple reports of trees down on homes, numerous trees and limbs down, and numerous wires down. Damage largely occurred between 530 PM and 600 PM.
Jefferson County Emergency Management reports multiple trees down across the Menan and Lewisville area. Time estimated based on radar and surrounding reports.

Updated...Jefferson County Emergency Management reports multiple trees down in Menan and Lewisville with widespread debris across the area, power out, and flooding. One road damaged by a debris flow near Menan Butte. Local fire departments and road and bridge crews are out clearing trees that are blocking roads. Time estimated based on radar and surrounding reports.
Tree uprooted in Menan. Time estimated based on radar and surrounding reports.
Rocky Mountain Power reports over 7,000 customers without power in the area.

4 trees snapped off and down at the intersection of E 700 N and N 3700 E, with several pieces of siding removed from residence and small twigs and branches impaled into the siding on one side of the residence. One tree down on a neighbors roof. Time estimated based on radar and surrounding reports.
Small tree limbs reported down in Lewisville.

Livestock outbuilding constructed of wooden beams, wooden poles, and sheet metal destroyed along N 3800 E just south of E 800 N. Multiple trees and large tree limbs down in the area. Damage all laying to the south, southeast, and east. Time estimated based on radar.

Report of widespread storm damage from Menan, to Annis, to Lorenzo along Menan Lorenzo Highway. Multiple trees down, damage to residential siding, power lines down. Time estimated based on radar and surrounding reports.
Tree snapped off and down at Annis Little Butte Cemetery. Report and photo received via social media. Time estimated based on radar and surrounding reports.
Large tree down along E Menan Lorenzo Highway, laying southeast. Time estimated based on radar.

Two very large trees uprooted and laying to the southeast along N 3950 E, one measured at 4 feet in diameter, one measured at 3.5 feet in diameter, along with 1 power pole down. At least 7 additional trees down along Long Island Canal. Local farmer reports approximately 20 sheep killed due to falling trees in this area. Time estimated based on radar.

10 power poles down, numerous power pole cross arms broken, and at least 15 large trees down, some snapped at various heights and some uprooted, along E 600 N between N 3950 E and N 4062 E. One large tree fell through the bedroom of a residence with no injuries. All damage laying east or southeast. Multiple roads in the area blocked. Time estimated based on radar.

Large tree over 2 feet in diameter snapped off several feet above the ground and down on top of a dump truck. Other debris strewn across the area. Report and photos received via social media. Time estimated based on radar and surrounding reports.

Corridor of significant wind damage stretching from just northwest of Annis...southeast between Rigby and Lorenzo...ending between North Yellowstone Highway and the Labelle community. NWS storm survey estimates damaging thunderstorm outflow winds of 60 MPH and a wet microburst with winds of 65 to 80 MPH occurred. Within this corridor an estimated 50 to 100 trees were either snapped off or uprooted...multiple trees fell on top of or through homes with varying degrees of damage...the roofs of several barns and outbuildings were damaged or blown off...at least a dozen vehicles were significantly damaged and approximately 20 sheep were killed due to falling trees...power poles were damaged...multiple roads were blocked...and power outages were widespread.

Multiple large trees uprooted in the area of E 600 N and N 4100 E. Trees down on at least two homes with a hole in the side of one home. No injuries reported. Report and photos received via social media. Time estimated based on radar and surrounding reports.
Large tree down partially destroying a trailer home. Residents were inside at the time of damage and were not injured. Time estimated.
Rocky Mountain Power reports over 900 customers without power in the Sugar City area due to thunderstorm winds.
Large tree limbs reported down at Jefferson County Lake/Rigby Lake. Time estimated based on radar and surrounding reports.
Rocky Mountain Power reports over 10,000 customers without power in the area.

Updated...Area of significant tree damage with numerous trees down and wires down at the intersection of N Yellowstone Highway and E 550 N. Initial photos received via social media. NWS Storm Survey found at least 4 large trees measured at 3 to 4 feet in diameter uprooted and down across Yellowstone Highway with multiple other smaller trees down across the area. All damage laying southeast. Time estimated based on radar.
Large tree limbs down in the Labelle area, along with leaf litter in yards. Time estimated based on radar.
Multiple vehicles damaged by falling trees at a wedding at The Millhouse Venue, with significant dents and smashed windshields reported. Time estimated.

At least 5-6 trees down at Jefferson Hills Golf Course, with photos of additional trees down at nearby residences, one down across a wood stockade fence. Time estimated based on radar and surrounding reports.
Area of significant tree damage with numerous trees down and wires down at the intersection of N Yellowstone Highway and E 550 N. Photos received via social media. Time estimated.
Many branches down, some as large as 3 inches in diameter, and significant standing water reported after storms exited the area. Time estimated based on radar and surrounding reports.

*** 4 INJ *** Boat caught on Palisades Reservoir as severe thunderstorm moved overhead. Video captured heavy hail falling with some stones larger than quarters resulting in dents to the boat. 4 occupants received bruises from the hail. No other injuries. Time estimated based on radar.

*** 2 INJ *** At least two additional injuries reported from another group caught out on Palisades Reservoir during the severe thunderstorm. Group took shelter under a surfboard and life jackets in the water, but received bruises and welts from the hail. Time estimated.

Time estimated based on radar and power outage start times.
